important_relationships.relates_to: Switch, router, firewall, access point, controller and appliance hardware
important_relationships.why_it_matters: Network software support is often constrained by exact device model, ASIC, line card, supervisor, CPU architecture and hardware revision.

important_relationships.relates_to: Optics, transceivers, cables, NICs, DPUs and interface modules
important_relationships.why_it_matters: Link speed, breakout, FEC, DOM, distance and protocol support depend on validated device and software combinations.

important_relationships.relates_to: Controllers, managers, orchestrators and telemetry agents
important_relationships.why_it_matters: Management platforms often support only specific network OS releases, device families, plugins, agents and adaptor versions.

important_relationships.relates_to: Peer network operating systems, routing protocols and fabric components
important_relationships.why_it_matters: EVPN-VXLAN, BGP, OSPF, MPLS, SD-WAN, wireless and fabric deployments can depend on versioned peer behavior and supported features.

important_relationships.relates_to: Target software release and upgrade path
important_relationships.why_it_matters: Network upgrades can require intermediate releases, firmware or bootloader changes, synchronized controller versions and feature migrations.

important_relationships.why_it_matters: Upgrades can change kernel support, runtime APIs, partitioning behavior, peer-to-peer paths and certified server combinations.
